parts.prompt: How many cubic feet of rainwater flow into the pipe during the 8-hour time interval 0 (cid:133) t (cid:133) 8?

scoring_points.description: Correct estimate or setup with supporting work

parts.prompt:
Is the amount of water in the pipe increasing or decreasing at time t (cid:32) 3 hours? Give a reason for your
answer.

scoring_points.description: Correct numerical answer or critical value

parts.prompt: At what time t, 0 (cid:133) t (cid:133) 8, is the amount of water in the pipe at a minimum? Justify your answer.

scoring_points.description: Correct identification of extremum or critical value

required_elements: Critical point analysis or sign change

scoring_points.description: Correct justification using first derivative test

parts.prompt:
The pipe can hold 50 cubic feet of water before overflowing. For t (cid:33) 8, water continues to flow into and out
of the pipe at the given rates until the pipe begins to overflow. Write, but do not solve, an equation involving
one or more integrals that gives the time w when the pipe will begin to overflow.

scoring_points.description: Correct average value or rate integral
